proposed_hypotheses.text: objective_response is a binary outcome with positive prevalence between 10% and 50% in the cohort.
feature_030 carries no information because it is constant across all 50000 patients.

analyses.result_summary: objective_response is binary with 24.0% prevalence (12011/50000 events).
feature_030 has only 1 unique value (all 0). Not usable in any analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 32 features, several are binary indicators (likely diagnoses, comorbidities, or treatments) and several are continuous laboratory measures (likely PSA, hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, etc.).

analyses.code: for c in feature_cols: print(c, df[c].nunique(), df[c].dtype)
analyses.result_summary: 12 binary features (013,006,021,015,027,005,008,023,011,017,019,004); 2 multilevel (001 with 3 levels, 010 with 5 levels 6-10); and 17 continuous features. feature_016 (mean 65.0, range 30-90) is consistent with age; feature_022 (mean 42.7, range 0.12-3622) is consistent with PSA; feature_026 (mean 12.5, range 6-18) is consistent with hemoglobin.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 has a positive univariate association with objective_response (response rate higher when feature_008==1).
feature_013 has a negative univariate association with objective_response (response rate lower when feature_013==1).
Continuous PSA-like feature_022 is negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_008==1 vs 0: log-odds change +1.097 (OR=2.99), p<1e-300. Response rate 36.1% (T=1) vs 15.9% (T=0).
feature_013==1 vs 0: log-odds change -1.071 (OR=0.34), p<1e-300.
feature_022 (per unit): log-odds -0.0047 per unit, p=1.6e-94.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features feature_015, feature_021, and feature_027 are independently negatively associated with objective_response.
feature_001 (3 levels, 0/1/2, possibly stage) is negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: univariate logistic for each binary feature
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 univariate coef -0.615 (OR=0.54, p=2e-96); feature_021 -0.640 (OR=0.53, p=1.5e-54); feature_027 -0.403 (OR=0.67, p=3.1e-9). All three significantly negative.
feature_001 coef per level: -0.285 (OR=0.75 per level, p=9.5e-73). Stage-like ordinal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full multivariable adjustment, the strongest independent positive predictor of objective_response is feature_008 and the strongest negative predictors are feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_001, and feature_027.

The pattern (one binary feature with strong positive adjusted effect; others negative) is consistent with feature_008 being a treatment indicator and the negative-effect features being adverse disease/host characteristics.

analyses.code: Multivariable logistic regression on all features (continuous standardized)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients with smallest p-values: feature_013 -1.126 (p~0); feature_008 +1.201 (p~0); feature_015 -0.694 (p=1e-108); feature_001 -0.328 (p=1e-82); feature_021 -0.735 (p=5e-64); feature_020 -0.140 std (p=2e-33); feature_027 -0.431 (p=3e-9); feature_002 +0.064 std (p=2e-8); feature_022 -0.097 std (p=3e-8); feature_024 -0.068 std (p=9e-8). Other features non-significant after adjustment.

analyses.code: Inspection of effect sizes and prevalence patterns
analyses.result_summary: feature_008 has 40.2% prevalence and is the only binary feature with a strong positive adjusted effect. Other binary features either have negative effects (013, 015, 021, 027) or small/null effects (006, 005, 023, 011, 017, 019, 004). This is consistent with feature_008 being the only effective therapy in the panel.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_008 on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient subgroups; specifically the treatment effect is smaller (or absent) in patients with feature_013==1 (interaction term in feature_008 x feature_013 logistic model is negative and significant).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.978, p<1e-300. Treatment-effect log-odds = +2.04 in feature_013==0 patients vs +0.06 in feature_013==1 patients. Treatment effect is essentially abolished by feature_013==1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_008 is also abolished by feature_015==1 (negative significant interaction).
Treatment effect of feature_008 is also abolished by feature_021==1 (negative significant interaction).
Treatment effect of feature_008 is also abolished by feature_027==1 (negative significant interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction = -1.237, p=1.4e-93. Treatment-effect log-odds +1.30 (feature_015==0) vs +0.063 (feature_015==1).
Interaction = -1.138, p=3.3e-42. Treatment effect +1.19 vs +0.05.
Interaction = -1.283, p=1.8e-19. Treatment effect +1.13 vs -0.15 (potentially harmful in feature_027==1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_008 decreases as continuous feature_022 (PSA-like) increases (negative significant interaction).
Treatment effect of feature_008 increases with feature_001 stage level (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction (per SD of feature_022) = -0.805, p=1.2e-72. Treatment effect log-odds +1.80 at -1 SD vs +0.19 at +1 SD.
Interaction = +0.141 per level, p=1.6e-5. Treatment effect log-odds +0.86 at level 0 vs +1.14 at level 2. Modest positive modification — treatment relatively more beneficial at higher stage levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the 16 joint binary subgroups defined by feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027, the treatment effect of feature_008 is concentrated in the subgroup where all four are 0; in every other binary combination the treatment effect is approximately null.

analyses.code: for combo in product([0,1],4): compute response rates by treatment within combo
analyses.result_summary: Within (013=0,015=0,021=0,027=0) cell (n=15681): response 79.8% on T=1 vs 17.2% on T=0 (diff=+62.6 pts, OR~19). All 15 other binary combinations show treatment-effect differences between -12 and +9 percentage points, most centered near 0. This is a stark cliff — treatment benefit is essentially 0/1 across this 4-feature partition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining the favorable subgroup as feature_013==0 AND feature_015==0 AND feature_021==0 AND feature_027==0 (n=15681, 31% of cohort), the treatment effect of feature_008 yields response 79.8% (T=1) vs 17.2% (T=0); in the complement (n=34319) the difference is 16.1% vs 15.3% (a difference of ~1 pt).
A formal logistic model with treatment x favorable-subgroup interaction will show a near-zero main treatment effect and a very large positive interaction term.

analyses.result_summary: favorable: T=0 17.2%, T=1 79.8%, diff +62.6 pts; non-favorable: T=0 15.3%, T=1 16.1%, diff +0.8 pts.
T main = +0.058 (p=0.055, OR 1.06); fav main = +0.141 (p=4e-5); T:fav interaction = +2.885 (p<1e-300, OR multiplier 17.9). T-effect log-odds in favorable = 2.94, OR ~19.

proposed_hypotheses.text: All four features (013, 015, 021, 027) are necessary in the favorable-subgroup definition: removing any single excluder dilutes the within-subgroup treatment-effect difference.

analyses.code: For each excluder, drop it from the favorable definition and recompute T-effect
analyses.result_summary: Full def diff = +0.626. Drop feature_013=0: diff falls to +0.287 (largest dilution). Drop feature_015=0: diff +0.505. Drop feature_021=0: diff +0.563. Drop feature_027=0: diff +0.607. Smallest contribution is feature_027=0 but all four dilute the effect when removed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding more excluders monotonically increases the within-favorable T-effect: 1-feature defs give diff ~0.21-0.44, 2-feature defs ~0.25-0.55, 3-feature defs ~0.29-0.61, 4-feature def 0.626.

analyses.result_summary: Single-excluder T-effects: feature_013=0 +0.440, feature_015=0 +0.251, feature_021=0 +0.225, feature_027=0 +0.209. Two-way: best is feature_013=0 & feature_015=0 +0.546. Three-way: best is feature_013=0 & feature_015=0 & feature_021=0 +0.607. Four-way (full): +0.626. Each addition is monotonic. Outside each subgroup, the T-effect remains near 0 (~0.005-0.010), indicating a sharp threshold.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, no other binary feature (006, 005, 023, 011, 017, 019, 004) further modifies the feature_008 treatment effect — treatment-effect differences across these splits remain near +0.62 in all strata.

analyses.code: Within fav, for each binary feature, compute T-effect in each stratum
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable, T-effects by other binary stratum: feature_006 (0:+0.626, 1:+0.623); feature_005 (0:+0.648, 1:+0.611); feature_023 (0:+0.626, 1:+0.626); feature_011 (0:+0.629, 1:+0.618); feature_017 (0:+0.630, 1:+0.585); feature_019 (0:+0.622, 1:+0.646); feature_004 (0:+0.624, 1:+0.658). All differences within ±0.04 — no meaningful additional heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the treatment effect does not vary materially across feature_001 levels (0/1/2) or feature_010 levels (Gleason 6-10).

analyses.code: Stratify favorable by feature_001 then feature_010, compute T-effect
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable: feature_001 levels 0/1/2 yield T-effects +0.646/+0.622/+0.590 (slight monotone decrease). feature_010 levels 6-10 yield +0.629/+0.621/+0.635/+0.615/+0.634 (essentially flat). No clinically meaningful additional modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, feature_022 (continuous, PSA-like) is a prognostic factor (negative association with response) but NOT a treatment-effect modifier — i.e., its negative effect on response is similar in both treatment arms.

analyses.code: Within fav, fit Logit y ~ zfeature_022 separately by T
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable, per-SD coefficient of feature_022: -0.082 (p=0.0088) in T=0; -0.092 (p=0.0013) in T=1. Coefficients are nearly identical, ruling out an interaction inside the favorable subgroup. The previously observed feature_008 x feature_022 interaction is therefore confounded by correlation between feature_022 and the four binary excluders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the non-favorable subgroup (any of feature_013/015/021/027 = 1), there is no sub-subgroup where feature_008 produces a substantial benefit — top interaction terms inside non-favorable are at best p~0.004 and produce only minor effect-size differences.

analyses.code: Within non-fav, screen interactions of T with every other feature
analyses.result_summary: Top within-non-favorable interactions: feature_005 +0.177 (p=0.004); feature_025 +0.056 (p=0.064); others p>0.09. None reach Bonferroni-corrected significance, and none come close to the magnitude of the favorable-subgroup T-effect. Treatment is effectively useless once any of the four excluders is positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features (006, 005, 023, 011, 017, 019, 004) are NOT plausible treatment indicators because (a) they have null or trivial main effects on response after adjustment and (b) they show no heterogeneity by the same modifiers that strongly modify feature_008.

analyses.code: For each candidate, screen interactions with the four mods and feature_022
analyses.result_summary: feature_006/005/023/011/017/019/004 all have main effects |coef|<0.05 (p>0.20) after adjustment. Interaction tests with the same four binary modifiers and feature_022 show no consistent strong signal — only feature_005:feature_027 (p=0.047) and feature_023:feature_021 (p=0.031) and feature_017:feature_021 (p=0.045) reach nominal significance, none survive Bonferroni and effect sizes are an order of magnitude smaller than feature_008 interactions. None of these features behaves like a therapy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with feature_008 is balanced across the favorable and non-favorable subgroups (no large confounding by indication on the binary excluder dimension).

analyses.result_summary: Treatment prevalence is 40.3% in favorable and 40.1% in non-favorable. Differences are negligible, so the dramatic treatment-effect heterogeneity cannot be explained by selection on the favorability indicator.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all other features in a multivariable model that includes T, fav, and T*fav, the T*fav interaction remains very large (>2 log-odds units) and highly significant, while the residual T main effect (in non-favorable) is essentially null.

analyses.code: Logit y ~ T + fav + T*fav + all other features (continuous standardized)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted T (in non-fav) coef = +0.061 (p=0.047, OR 1.06); fav main +0.101 (p=0.003); T*fav interaction = +2.945 (p~0). Within-favorable adjusted T log-odds = +3.006 (OR 20.2). Adjustment for the 27 other features does not attenuate the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (PSA-like) is independently negatively associated with objective_response after adjustment, as expected for a tumor burden marker (per-SD adjusted coef ~ -0.10, p<1e-7).
feature_001 (3-level, stage-like) is independently negatively associated with objective_response after adjustment (per-level adjusted coef ~ -0.33, p<1e-80).

analyses.result_summary: Per SD of feature_022, adjusted log-odds = -0.097 (p=3.3e-8), small but highly significant negative prognostic effect.
Per level of feature_001, adjusted log-odds = -0.328 (p=1.1e-82). Strong stage-like prognostic effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_020 is independently negatively associated with objective_response (continuous, per-SD adjusted log-odds ~ -0.14, p<1e-30) and feature_002 is independently positively associated (per-SD ~ +0.06, p<1e-7).

analyses.result_summary: feature_020 adjusted per-SD coef = -0.140 (p=1.7e-33). feature_002 adjusted per-SD = +0.064 (p=1.8e-8). Both are independent of treatment effect heterogeneity but are useful prognostic signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_024 is independently negatively associated with objective_response (per-SD adjusted log-odds ~ -0.07, p<1e-6).

analyses.result_summary: feature_024 adjusted per-SD coef = -0.068, p=8.6e-8. Modest independent negative prognostic effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, none of the prognostic features that retain modest associations (feature_001 stage, feature_022 PSA-like, feature_020) modifies the treatment effect; their effect on response is approximately equal in T=0 and T=1 patients.

analyses.code: Within favorable, fit Logit y ~ feature_001 separately by T; same for feature_022, feature_020
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 within fav: T=0 coef -0.351 (p=4e-17), T=1 coef -0.464 (p=8e-24); difference is small. feature_022 within fav: T=0 -0.082, T=1 -0.092 (essentially equal). These features remain prognostic but not predictive within the favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final summary: only feature_008 functions as an effective treatment in this cohort, and its benefit is essentially restricted to the joint subgroup of patients with feature_013==0 AND feature_015==0 AND feature_021==0 AND feature_027==0.

analyses.code: Combined evidence above; absolute response rates and adjusted ORs
analyses.result_summary: Within the favorable subgroup (n=15681, 31% of cohort): T=1 response 79.8% vs T=0 response 17.2% (absolute benefit 62.6 percentage points; OR 19.0 unadjusted, 20.2 adjusted). In the non-favorable complement (n=34319): T=1 16.1% vs T=0 15.3% (absolute benefit 0.8 pts; OR 1.06). Heterogeneity p<1e-300. No alternative single binary feature behaves like a treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Patients receiving feature_008 (treatment) have a substantially higher probability of objective_response than those not receiving feature_008 IF AND ONLY IF feature_013==0 AND feature_015==0 AND feature_021==0 AND feature_027==0; outside this joint subgroup the treatment confers no meaningful benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Final hypothesis supported by: (a) interaction tests in iters 6-8, (b) joint 16-cell table in iter 9, (c) necessity of all four excluders in iters 11-12, (d) absence of further modification within favorable in iters 13-15, (e) absence of any sub-subgroup of benefit within non-favorable in iter 16, (f) treatment-arm balance check in iter 18, (g) covariate-adjusted heterogeneity model in iter 19. Direction: positive (feature_008 increases response) in the favorable subgroup. Magnitude: +62.6 percentage points absolute, OR ~20.
